segments.text: The allotment for the tribe of Judah, according to its clans, extended down to the territory of Edom, to the Desert of Zin in the extreme south.
Their southern boundary started from the bay at the southern end of the Dead Sea,
crossed south of Scorpion Pass, continued on to Zin and went over to the south of Kadesh Barnea. Then it ran past Hezron up to Addar and curved around to Karka.
In accordance with the Lord's command to him, Joshua gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh a portion in Judah—Kiriath Arba, that is, Hebron. (Arba was the forefather of Anak.)
I will give my daughter Aksah in marriage to the man who attacks and captures Kiriath Sepher.
segments.speaker: Caleb

segments.text: One day when she came to Othniel, she urged him to ask her father for a field. When she got off her donkey, Caleb asked her,
What can I do for you?
segments.speaker: Caleb

segments.text: Do me a special favor. Since you have given me land in the Negev, give me also springs of water.
segments.speaker: Aksah

segments.text: So Caleb gave her the upper and lower springs.
Judah could not dislodge the Jebusites, who were living in Jerusalem; to this day the Jebusites live there with the people of Judah.
